description: Add .workflow case to SessionTab enum and remove .terminal case since the terminal becomes a permanent pane outside the tab bar
acceptance_criteria: SessionTab has five cases: .iterations, .files, .diff, .log, .workflow
The .terminal case is removed from the enum
All switch statements over SessionTab compile without the .terminal case
The app builds and runs without errors

description: Create TerminalDivider state model with collapse/expand, resize clamping, UserDefaults persistence, and unit tests
acceptance_criteria: Default terminal width is 320pt when no persisted value exists
Collapsing stores the current width and sets width to 0
Expanding restores the previously stored width
Dragging below 200pt clamps to 200pt
Dragging above 60 percent of window width clamps to 60 percent
Width and collapsed state persist across view re-renders via UserDefaults
All unit tests compile and pass

description: Create SidebarSessionStatus view that shows expanded status for the active session in the sidebar, with unit tests for data computation
acceptance_criteria: Shows state badge, phase name, iteration counter, task counter, and elapsed time
Iteration and task counters are only visible during the Build phase
Elapsed time is only visible when a run has started
Uses compact vertical layout that fits below a session row
Reads data from SessionRunStatus
Unit tests verify rendering for all five session states (idle, running, paused, completed, stalled)
Unit tests verify counters appear during Build phase and hide for other phases
All tests compile and pass

description: Create three-column HSplitView layout in SessionDetailView with terminal as permanent left pane and existing TabView as right pane, separated by TerminalDivider
acceptance_criteria: The session detail area shows terminal on the left and tabs on the right, separated by a draggable divider
The terminal is always visible by default alongside the active tab content
The divider shows a collapse button on hover and supports double-click to toggle
Pressing Cmd+backslash collapses the terminal pane to zero width and restores it to last-used width
The .inspector() modifier and inspectorPresented state are removed from SessionDetailView
The SessionHeaderStatus bar is removed from above the tabs
Minimum window size is increased to 900 by 500
The app builds and runs without errors

description: Create WorkflowTab view and integrate it into the right pane TabView, replacing the removed inspector drawer
acceptance_criteria: The Workflow tab appears in the tab bar and shows the workflow phase list with step rows
Each phase is expandable and shows its steps with status icons
The Mark Step Complete button is present and functional
The Run From Here button appears on pending steps
The view fills available width instead of the old 240pt inspector constraint
The ReviewPausePanel appears above the tab content when the workflow pauses on a review step
The old WorkflowInspector drawer is fully removed from the view hierarchy

description: Integrate SidebarSessionStatus into the sidebar for the active session row
acceptance_criteria: The active session row in the sidebar shows expanded status below the session name
Inactive session rows remain compact with no expanded status
The expanded status updates in real time as the session runs
The old SessionHeaderStatus is no longer rendered anywhere in the view hierarchy

description: Replace Toggle Inspector menu command with Toggle Terminal using Cmd+backslash and update tab keyboard shortcuts for the new five-tab layout
acceptance_criteria: The View menu shows Toggle Terminal with Cmd+backslash instead of Toggle Inspector with Ctrl+Cmd+I
Pressing Cmd+backslash collapses the terminal when expanded and expands it when collapsed
Cmd+1 through Cmd+5 switch between Iterations, Files, Diff, Log, and Workflow tabs respectively
No keyboard shortcut maps to the removed terminal tab case

description: Add colored event type badges (assistant/tool/tool-result) to each event line in the iteration transcript
acceptance_criteria: Each assistant text event shows a green 'assistant' badge pill
Each tool use event shows a blue 'tool' badge pill
Each tool result event shows a purple 'result' badge pill
Badges are compact (10px font, 2px radius, 4px horizontal padding) with colored background and text
Badge colors: assistant=#e8f5e9/#2e7d32, tool=#e3f2fd/#1565c0, result=#f3e5f5/#7b1fa2
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Iteration transcript events show colored text and arrows but no distinct badge pills for event types
context.what_expected: Each event line should have a small colored badge pill labeling the event type (assistant, tool, result) matching the prototype design
steps_to_reproduce: Open a session with completed iterations
Expand an iteration card to see the transcript
Observe event lines have no type badge, only colored text
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 571-581. EventLineView in IterationCardView.swift handles event rendering.

description: Change effort badge styling from gray tertiary capsule to purple pill matching the prototype design
acceptance_criteria: Effort badge uses purple background (#f3e5f5) and purple text (#7b1fa2)
Badge is a small pill (10px font, 4px radius, 6px horizontal padding)
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Effort badges on task groups use a gray tertiary capsule style
context.what_expected: Effort badges should use a purple pill style with light purple background and dark purple text
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Iterations tab
Look at task group headers with effort badges (S/M/L)
Observe they are gray instead of purple
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 477-484. TaskGroupView in IterationCardView.swift renders the effort badge.

description: Add status text badges (Completed/In Progress/Pending) to task group headers in the Iterations view
acceptance_criteria: Completed task groups show a green 'Completed' badge pill
Active task groups show a blue 'In Progress' badge pill
Pending task groups show a gray 'Pending' badge pill
Badges use 10px font, 4px radius, and match prototype color scheme
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Task group headers only show a status icon (checkmark/circle) with no text badge indicating the task state
context.what_expected: Each task group header should show a colored text badge (Completed/In Progress/Pending) next to the effort badge
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Iterations tab with a tasks.json that has completed, active, and pending tasks
Observe task group headers have no status text badge
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 467-476. TaskGroupView in IterationCardView.swift needs the badge added.

description: Add live indicator (pulsing green dot + 'N iterations (live)') to the active task group header in the Iterations view
acceptance_criteria: The active task group header shows a pulsing green dot next to the iteration count
The text reads 'N iteration(s) (live)' in green
The dot uses a 1.5s ease-in-out pulse animation cycling opacity between 1 and 0.4
The indicator only appears on the task group that contains the current iteration
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: The active task group header shows a plain iteration count with no live indicator
context.what_expected: The active task group header should show a pulsing green dot and '(live)' text next to the iteration count
steps_to_reproduce: Start a session running the Build phase
Open the Iterations tab
Observe the active task group header has no live indicator
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 906-909. TaskGroupView in IterationCardView.swift renders the header.

description: Dim pending task groups at 60 percent opacity in the Iterations view
acceptance_criteria: Pending task groups (no iterations yet) render at 60% opacity
Completed and in-progress task groups remain at full opacity
The dimming applies to the entire task group including header and any nested cards
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Pending task groups render at full opacity, same as active and completed groups
context.what_expected: Pending task groups should be visually de-emphasized at 60% opacity
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Iterations tab with tasks that have pending items
Observe pending task groups are at full opacity
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 939-950. TaskGroupView in IterationCardView.swift needs opacity modifier.

description: Style iteration number badge as blue text on light blue background instead of gray capsule
acceptance_criteria: Iteration number badge uses blue text (#007aff) on light blue background (#e3f2fd)
Badge has 4px radius and 8px horizontal padding
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Iteration number badges (Iter #1, etc.) use a gray capsule style
context.what_expected: Iteration number badges should use blue text on a light blue pill background
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Iterations tab with completed iterations
Observe iteration number badges are gray instead of blue
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 512-519. IterationCardView.swift renders the badge in the header.

description: Add dual line number columns (old/new) with marker column to the diff view
acceptance_criteria: Each diff line shows two number columns: old line number (40px) and new line number (40px)
A 16px marker column between the number columns shows '+', '-', or space
Added lines show only the new number column, removed lines show only the old number column
Context lines show both old and new numbers with the marker invisible
Line numbers are right-aligned, 11px font, tertiary color, non-selectable
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Diff lines only show a '+ '/ '- '/ '  ' prefix with no line numbers
context.what_expected: Each diff line should have two number columns (old/new) with a marker column, matching the prototype's full diff layout
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Diff tab with changes to view
Observe diff lines have no line number columns
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/02-diff.html lines 250-274. SessionDiffLineRow in SessionDiffView.swift renders each line.

description: Style diff hunk headers with blue background and blue text matching the prototype
acceptance_criteria: Hunk headers use blue background (#f1f8ff) and blue text (#0366d6)
Hunk headers use monospaced 11px font
Hunk headers have 6px vertical and 14px horizontal padding
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Hunk headers render as plain secondary text with no background color
context.what_expected: Hunk headers should have a light blue background with blue monospaced text
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Diff tab with changes that have hunk headers
Observe hunk headers are plain text without blue styling
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/02-diff.html lines 241-244. SessionDiffFileView in SessionDiffView.swift renders hunk headers.

description: Show aggregate diff stats (+additions/-removals) next to the scope picker in the diff header
acceptance_criteria: Total additions shown in green (#22863a) with '+' prefix, right-aligned in the header
Total removals shown in red (#cb2431) with '-' prefix, next to additions
Stats use 11px monospaced font
Stats update when scope changes between 'Last commit' and 'Full session'
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: The diff header only shows the scope picker with no aggregate change statistics
context.what_expected: The diff header should show total additions and removals counts next to the scope picker
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Diff tab with changes
Observe no stats are shown next to the scope segmented control
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/02-diff.html lines 403-406. SessionDiffView.swift renders the header.

description: Show per-file addition/removal counts in each diff file header
acceptance_criteria: Each file header shows per-file addition count in green and removal count in red
Stats are right-aligned in the file header, 10px monospaced font
New files show only additions, deleted files show only removals
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Diff file headers show only the file path with no per-file change statistics
context.what_expected: Each file header should show per-file addition and removal counts
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Diff tab with multiple changed files
Observe file headers show no per-file stats
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/02-diff.html lines 413-416. SessionDiffFileView in SessionDiffView.swift renders file headers.

description: Add workflow header with 'Workflow Progress' title and session name subtitle to the Workflow tab
acceptance_criteria: The Workflow tab shows a 'Workflow Progress' title at 16px semibold weight
Below the title, the session name is shown at 12px in secondary color
Header has 20px bottom margin
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: The Workflow tab jumps straight into the phase list with no header
context.what_expected: The Workflow tab should show a 'Workflow Progress' title and session name subtitle above the phase list
steps_to_reproduce: Select a session and open the Workflow tab
Observe no header is shown above the phase list
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/03-workflow.html lines 415-418. WorkflowTab.swift renders the content.

description: Show step count summary (N of M steps completed) under each phase name in the Workflow tab
acceptance_criteria: Each phase header shows a step count line like '3 of 3 steps completed' or '0 of 2 steps'
The count text is 11px secondary color, shown below the phase name
The count reflects actual completed vs total steps for that phase
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Phase headers only show the phase name with no step count summary
context.what_expected: Each phase header should show 'N of M steps completed' below the phase name
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Workflow tab on a session with workflow progress
Observe phase headers have no step count line
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/03-workflow.html lines 426-427. InspectorPhaseSection renders phase headers.

description: Add status badges (Completed/Current/Pending) to phase headers in the Workflow tab
acceptance_criteria: Completed phases show a green 'Completed' badge
The current phase shows a blue 'Current' badge
Pending phases show a gray 'Pending' badge
Badges use 10px font, 4px radius, 8px horizontal padding, 2px vertical padding
Badge colors: completed=#e8f5e9/#2e7d32, current=#e3f2fd/#1565c0, pending=#f5f5f5/#757575
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Phase headers show only a status icon with no text badge indicating the phase state
context.what_expected: Each phase header should show a colored text badge (Completed/Current/Pending)
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Workflow tab on a session with workflow progress
Observe phase headers have no status text badge
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/03-workflow.html lines 207-212. InspectorPhaseSection renders phase headers.

description: Add step descriptions below step names in the Workflow tab step rows
acceptance_criteria: Each step row shows a description line below the step display name
Description is 11px secondary color text
Description is indented 28px to align with step content
Steps without descriptions show nothing (no empty space)
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: Step rows only show the step display name with no description
context.what_expected: Each step row should show a description line (e.g., 'Interview and refine requirements') below the step name
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Workflow tab on a session
Observe step rows show only the name with no description
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/03-workflow.html lines 238-239. InspectorStepRow renders step rows. Step descriptions would need to come from the Workflow model or be derived from the step type.

description: Style the Mark Complete keyboard shortcut as a styled badge inside the button
acceptance_criteria: The keyboard shortcut text is shown as a pill badge with semi-transparent white background
Badge uses 10px monospaced font
Badge has 1px vertical and 6px horizontal padding, 3px radius
Badge opacity is 0.7
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: The Mark Complete button shows keyboard shortcut as plain text without badge styling
context.what_expected: The shortcut text should be rendered as a styled pill badge matching the prototype design
steps_to_reproduce: Open the Workflow tab
Look at the 'Mark step as complete' button at the bottom
Observe the shortcut text is plain rather than a styled badge
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/03-workflow.html lines 276-279. WorkflowTab.swift renders the button.

description: Add terminal header bar with icon, title showing the agent name, and restart button
acceptance_criteria: Terminal pane shows a 36px header bar at the top with dark background (#2d2d2d)
Header shows a keyboard icon and title text like 'Terminal — claude'
Header shows a restart button (⟳) on the right side
Restart button has hover state with light background
Header has a subtle bottom border (0.5px, rgba white 0.06)
The app builds and runs without errors

context.what_happened: The terminal pane has no header bar — the terminal content starts immediately
context.what_expected: The terminal pane should have a header bar showing the agent name and a restart button
steps_to_reproduce: Select a session
Look at the terminal pane on the left
Observe no header bar above the terminal content
context.notes: Reference prototype: prototypes/ui-redesign/01-iterations.html lines 264-306. TerminalHost.swift wraps the terminal view.
